description: Letter from a man to his sister Umm Bū l-ʿAlāʾ. Sent care of Eliyyahu the Judge. In the hand of a scribe. In Judaeo-Arabic. Dating: Early 13th century. The letter contains an extremely vivid account of the imprisonment and tortures to which he and Abū Saʿd were subjected. (Information in part from Mediterranean Society, II, p. 609; III, pp. 43, 432). Discussed in Esther-Miriam Wagner, "‘Only death remains for him’, T-S 10J7.4" (Fragment of the Month, May 2011). Same scribe as ENA NS 48.26 (likewise an account of torture). ASE.
tags: personal, torture, Illness, address as seal, illness letter 969-1517, prison, mysterious mark
description_he: מכתב מאיש לאחותו אום בו אל-עלאא, בתיווך אליהו השופט. מכיל תיאור חי ביותר של מאסרו ועינויים שסבלו הוא ואבו סעד.
